sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, value DECIMAL(10, 5), description VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value, description) VALUES (1, 17.4, 'Test Case 1'), (2, NULL, 'Test Case 2'), (3, 25.99, 'Test Case 3'), (4, 0.001, 'Test Case 4'), (5, -5, 'Test Case 5');
SELECT id, ceiling(value) AS rounded_value, ~id AS negated_id FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY negated_id;
CREATE TABLE temp_data AS SELECT id, ceiling(value) AS ceiling_value FROM test_data WHERE description LIKE '%Test%';
SELECT id, ceiling(~value) AS negated_ceiling_value FROM test_data WHERE description IS NULL OR description <> '';
WITH subquery AS (SELECT id, value FROM test_data WHERE value < 0) SELECT id, ceiling(~value) AS negated_ceiling FROM subquery;
SELECT id, ceiling(value) + ~id AS complex_calculation FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING complex_calculation < 20;
SELECT id, ceiling(value) AS rounded_value FROM (SELECT id, value FROM test_data) AS derived WHERE rounded_value > 5;
CREATE TABLE special_cases (num_str VARCHAR, num_val DECIMAL);
INSERT INTO special_cases (num_str, num_val) VALUES ('17', 17.0), ('-1.5', -1.5), ('NULL', NULL), ('', 0);
SELECT num_str, ceiling(~CAST(num_str AS DECIMAL)) AS ceiling_negated_value FROM special_cases WHERE num_str IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, ceiling(value) + 10 AS adjusted_value FROM test_data WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_data WHERE value IS NULL) ORDER BY adjusted_value DESC;
